conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That's understandable. So, how was your week? Any notable events or routines that stand out?
It's been a bit of a struggle, to be honest. Work's been piling up, and I feel like I'm drowning in spreadsheets.
Spreadsheets can be overwhelming. Can you tell me more about what's been going on at work that's got you feeling stuck?
Yeah, just too much responsibility, I guess. Our team leader is out sick, so all the tasks are piling up on me.
That sounds like a lot of pressure. Do you find yourself beating yourself up over not being able to keep up with everything?
Honestly? Yeah, it's been eating away at me. I feel guilty for not meeting expectations, even though it's not my fault the team leader is out.
It sounds like guilt is playing a big role in your feelings right now. Can you walk me through what that feels like in your body?
It's this heavy weight in my chest, like I'm carrying around a load of shame or something. Sometimes it even makes my hands shake a bit.
That sounds really uncomfortable. Have you been doing anything to try and cope with these feelings?
Um, not really. Just trying to power through, hoping things get better soon.
I see. It might be helpful to explore some coping strategies together. What do you think is the most enjoyable part of your day right now?
Honestly? I don't know... Maybe just getting home from work and collapsing onto my couch.
That sounds like a nice little escape. Do you have any hobbies or activities that bring you joy outside of work?
Yeah, I love to paint in my free time. It's a way for me to unwind and express myself creatively.
That's beautiful. What do you find most enjoyable about painting?
Just the feeling of letting go and losing myself in the process. It's like my worries just melt away for a little while.
That sounds incredibly therapeutic. Have you been making time for painting lately?
Honestly? No, not as much as I'd like. Work's taken over, so it feels like I've lost that little slice of me.
It can be tough to prioritize self-care when responsibilities pile up. Let's explore some ways we can work on carving out time for your creative pursuits.
Yeah, that sounds amazing. Just knowing someone is listening and understanding makes me feel a bit better already.
That means a lot to me too. What do you think you'd like to accomplish in our next session?
Honestly? I guess just being able to talk more about this guilt and shame without judgment would be great. It feels like it's eating away at me from the inside out.
We can definitely work on that. I'm here to support you, not judge you. How does that sound?
That sounds... okay, yeah, that's a good place to start.
So, we've got some groundwork laid for next time. Before we wrap up today, is there anything specific that's been bothering you or causing stress?
Yeah, just the constant pressure to perform at work and feeling like I'm failing if things don't go perfectly.

That sounds really frustrating. Remember, we'll be exploring ways to cope with this in our next session. For now, let's just take a deep breath and acknowledge that it's okay not to have all the answers right away.
Yeah... Okay, I think I can do that.
Great! Just one more question before we close out today: What are you hoping to get out of our sessions together?
Honestly? I just want to stop feeling like I'm stuck in this rut and start moving forward again. Maybe find some peace or closure, even if it's just a little.
That sounds very achievable. We'll work on that together. Thanks for opening up today; it means a lot to me.
Yeah, thanks... It feels good to finally talk about this stuff.
Of course! Remember, everything we discussed here is completely confidential. You can take as much time as you need to process and reflect before our next session.
Alright... Thanks again for listening.
